name: Fish in a Fish
recipeIngredient: 31 ounces Canned red salmon, drained (reserve liquid)
(Use with salmon liquid to make 1-1/2 cups total)
recipeInstructions:
Pastry: Toss flour with a spoon and lightly level each cup. Combine flour and salt in a large bowl. With a pastry blender, cut shortening until the mixture resembles small peas. Add cold water, tossing with a fork until the dough clings together. Press firmly into a ball and divide in half.
Filling: In a 2-quart saucepan, melt margarine and sauté onion until clear. Add rice, stirring to coat. Add 1-1/2 cups water, cover, and steam over low heat until tender (about 20 minutes). Stir in parsley, cool, cover, and chill. Remove bones and skin from canned salmon and break into large flakes.
Sauce: Make a roux with margarine and flour. Stir in liquid and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thickened. Stir in dill weed, season salt, and shrimp. Cool.
To assemble: Draw a fish shape on light cardboard to fit diagonally on a large baking sheet. Roll out pastry and cut out the fish shape. Place it on the sheet. Spread rice mixture within 1 inch of the edge. Arrange fish over rice. Spoon sauce over salmon. Roll out remaining pastry to cover, gently place over fish, and moisten the edge with ice water. Press the top crust to seal. Cut excess pastry from around the edge. Create fish scales by pressing a fork. Using a knife, cut slits for mouth and an X for eye. Brush with beaten egg. Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes, then at 375°F for 40-45 minutes until golden brown. Slide off the end of the tray onto a serving platter (may need to cool slightly to firm crust before moving). Garnish with lemon slices and parsley, and place a green olive slice for the eye. Serves 10-12.
